dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4233 IS: Save Struggling Hospitals Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Warner (for himself, Mrs. Blackburn, Mrs. Hyde-Smith, and Mr. Tuberville) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend title XVIII of the Social Security Act to codify the Medicare low-wage index hospital policy.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Save Struggling Hospitals Act.

section.header: Codification of the Medicare low-wage index hospital policy
section.text: Section 1886(d)(3)(E) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395ww(d)(3)(E)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (i), in the first sentence, by striking or (iv) and inserting , (iv), or (v); and
by adding at the end the following new clause:

clause.header: Area wage index adjustment for low-wage hospitals

subclause.text: For discharges occurring on or after October 1, 2019, the area wage index applicable under this subparagraph for a fiscal year to a hospital with an area wage index (determined without regard to this clause) below the 25th percentile area wage index shall be increased by ½ of the difference between the otherwise applicable final area wage index for such fiscal year for such hospital and the 25th percentile area wage index for such fiscal year across all hospitals.
Pursuant to the fourth sentence of clause (i), this clause shall be applied in a budget neutral manner. Any adjustments made for the purposes of such application of budget neutrality may not—

item.text: decrease the area wage index applicable under this subparagraph to a hospital with an area wage index (determined without regard to this clause) below the 75th percentile area wage index for the fiscal year; or

result in an area wage index applicable under this subparagraph to a hospital for such fiscal year that is less than 95 percent of the area wage index applicable under this subparagraph to such hospital for the preceding fiscal year.
